charged with running machine

Mrs. E. GurIey Flynn Jones, the
famous leader of the Industrial
Workers of the World and known
as the modern Joan of Arc, is to
visit Spokane again in the near
future, probably this week, to de-
liver lectures before meetings con.
Quoted by the local !. w. w. organ
ization. Mrs. Jones' visit to Spo-
kane last spring will be remem-
bered by Spokane citizens in gen.
eral, she having been tried on a
conspiracy charge at that time.


Charged with stealing a trunk
containing silk dresses belonging
to Miss Bessie Shaw. who rooms at
the Reliance hotel, Mr.. and Mrs.
Therriault were arrested yesterday
morning at 1880 o'clock as they
were waiting for a train to convey
them to Montana.


The funeral of J. M. Benedum,
the rancher who through despond-
ency over the loss of his fortune in
Idaho mines committed suiside on
his farm near Vera Friday, was
held yesterday afternoon.


Two valuable collie dogs, one Of
which was worth $500, were stolen
from the Glen Tana dairy barn yes
terday morning.
